# debtvalidationletter.org

> Free guide to debt validation letters under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA).

debtvalidationletter.org is a free, open-source educational resource about your right to demand debt validation from collectors under 15 U.S.C. Section 1692g (FDCPA Section 809). Part of the Open Bankruptcy Project.

## Pages

- https://debtvalidationletter.org/: Complete guide to debt validation letters -- what they are, when to send one, and why they matter.
- https://debtvalidationletter.org/template.html: Free debt validation letter template -- fill-in-the-blank, copy and send. No signup required.
- https://debtvalidationletter.org/fdcpa-rights.html: Your rights under FDCPA Section 809 -- the federal law requiring collectors to validate debts on request.
- https://debtvalidationletter.org/30-day-window.html: The 30-day validation window explained -- when the clock starts, what happens if you miss it, and how to protect yourself.
- https://debtvalidationletter.org/what-collectors-must-prove.html: What debt collectors must prove when you request validation -- amount, original creditor, chain of ownership, and more.
- https://debtvalidationletter.org/after-validation.html: What to do after you send a debt validation letter -- next steps, timelines, and what to watch for.
- https://debtvalidationletter.org/invalid-debt.html: When a debt cannot be validated -- your options, including whether you even need to consider bankruptcy.
- https://debtvalidationletter.org/common-mistakes.html: Common mistakes people make with debt validation letters -- and how to avoid them.
- https://debtvalidationletter.org/faq.html: Frequently asked questions about debt validation letters and FDCPA rights.

## Key Facts

- Under 15 U.S.C. Section 1692g, debt collectors must provide written validation notice within 5 days of first contact
- Consumers have 30 days from receiving the notice to dispute the debt in writing
- If disputed within 30 days, the collector must cease collection until validation is provided
- Validation must include the amount of the debt, the name of the original creditor, and verification of the debt
- The right to request validation does not eliminate the debt, but it forces the collector to prove the debt is valid
- If a collector cannot validate a debt, it may not legally continue collection efforts on that debt
- Debt validation is often a critical first step before considering whether bankruptcy is necessary

## Network

Part of the Open Bankruptcy Project:
- 1328f.com -- Discharge eligibility screener
- 1328f.org -- Research platform
- 523a.org -- Nondischargeable debts
- nondischargeable.org -- Guide to nondischargeable debts
- automaticstay.org -- The automatic stay
- meanstest.org -- Chapter 7 means test
- 341meeting.org -- Meeting of creditors
- 727a8.com -- The 8-year discharge bar
- relieffromstay.org -- Relief from the automatic stay
- dischargeinjunction.com -- The discharge injunction
- prosedebtors.org -- Resources for pro se debtors

## Contact

This site is maintained by volunteers. No legal advice is provided.
